opp.name: Honda_2025
opp.account: HONDA OF THE U.K. MANUFACTURING LIMITED
opp.next_step: Ch 30th - this is still on hold moving deal out Chased re project status and if RFP will be released March View: Early engagement, delayed project needs pushing and understanding as to why and when project will move. Deloitte has early engagement and pushing for s2p+clm and remove SAP. Bias towards Coupa. Deloitte confirmed internal politics but engaged and will work with us. Sent follow up 2/16/25 Will arrange catch up call in the New Year 2026. Received email back confirming RFP will be released in March Demo TBC (Zsofia will revert with dates)
opp.ais_why:
• Sole Avoma meeting on 18 Jul 2025 was an Introductory call with Zsofia Ambrus-Csetreki (Honda contact, currently at IBM) and an IBM partner co-attendee. No notes review possible without an additional call. Product scope has Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake ‚Äî meaningful AI product loadout but the only captured engagement is a single introductory call from 10 months ago.
• Qualified stage at $150K with BD_Engagement Unengaged. Single meeting from July 2025; LastActivity 2026-02-16 shows 3-month staleness. IBM SI involvement (Zsofia is at IBM). The IBM partnership channel suggests the deal is partner-led rather than direct-buyer-led, which typically dampens buyer-AI-engagement visibility.
• Low confidence: thin meeting evidence (one intro call), stale activity (3+ months), IBM partner-led. AI products in scope (Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) keep the score at AI Curious. Score 4 (boundary DOWN per rubric). Would move UP only with a direct Honda buyer engagement that surfaces AI use cases; would drop further with continued dormancy.

buyer.account_name: HONDA OF THE U.K. MANUFACTURING LIMITED

items.quote: My main stakeholder is the head of procurement, Greg James ... and the head of finance
we have a project, but we do not know what to expect
When the project will be continued I will contact you

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated clear qualitative pain but quantified almost nothing. On 18 Jul 2025 Zsofia Ambrus-Csetreki said the team runs approximately 250 sourcing events per year and handles them 'in an old school way', releasing RFPs by email, collecting proposals on SharePoint, and scoring live in meetings on a complex Excel scorecard. The one scope figure she gave is that only indirect sourcing requests above 100k pounds reach her team. No cycle-time, headcount, error-rate, or savings number was ever stated, and on budget she said 'we have a project, but we do not know what to expect'. Converting the 250-events-per-year volume and the manual-scoring effort into a quantified efficiency and ROI case is the open move.

champion.narrative: Zsofia Ambrus-Csetreki is an engaged, knowledgeable working-level contact and the de facto champion: she ran the discovery, has a clear requirements picture, said she wanted to bring the head of procurement into a demo, and was the source of the 8 Jun 2026 email that keeps a thread alive. However she is a mid-level Sourcing Lead (IT category) contracted through IBM, not a power sponsor, and she has not delivered the demo dates or escalated to the head of procurement she named. Her 8 Jun 2026 note ('When the project will be continued I will contact you') is a cooling, hold-the-line signal rather than active advocacy. The move is to give her a quantified business case as air cover and convert her into a sponsored channel to the head of procurement rather than relying on her alone.

competition.narrative: The principal competitive dynamic is the incumbent Coupa plus a strong 'do nothing / re-optimise Coupa' risk. On 18 Jul 2025 Zsofia confirmed Coupa is already in place for requisitions, orders and reporting but not used for sourcing because it was under-configured, and stressed the RFP's purpose is 'not to challenge Coupa' for P2P, only to add sourcing; Honda has not decided whether to keep Coupa. The Next Step note also records a 'bias towards Coupa' and that Deloitte has early engagement pushing an S2P+CLM agenda and to remove SAP, with confirmed internal politics, a system-integrator influence that could steer the buyer toward a Coupa-centric outcome. Our wedge is embedded sourcing automation, autonomous negotiation (Merlin / Agentic AI), and one integrated platform that dovetails with Coupa reporting and the intake form.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated and owned by the sourcing team. On 18 Jul 2025 Zsofia described sourcing running entirely manually: RFPs sent by email because suppliers cannot open Coupa links, proposals collected on SharePoint, and evaluations scored live in meetings on a complex Excel scorecard, with new Coupa access required each time an evaluator is added. Coupa was bought but under-configured ('we did not put enough energy into the configuration customization'), so it is not used for sourcing at all. The pain owner is Zsofia and the Budapest sourcing team supporting indirect sourcing across 18 European countries. This is confirmed, vivid operational pain; what is missing is the financial impact and an executive who feels it acutely enough to unblock the project.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been engaged. On 18 Jul 2025 Zsofia noted an NDA had been shared and acknowledged 'contracting sometimes takes weeks', which is why she originally targeted a year-end 2025 signature, but no MSA, SOW, legal, procurement-channel or InfoSec activity has followed in the ~11.7 months since, and no contract-expiration forcing function is on record. Honda's outsourcing of procurement to IBM adds an operating-model layer to any future paper process. What would create visibility is confirmation of the RFP release and the procurement/legal path once the project restarts.

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget authority is named by role but never engaged. On 18 Jul 2025 Zsofia named the head of procurement (UK) as her main stakeholder and the person who runs volume reports, plus the head of finance (UK) as a co-stakeholder, and said 'we have a project' with budget but 'we do not know what to expect'. Neither the head of procurement nor the head of finance is a Salesforce contact role, has ever joined a call, or has been contacted directly in ~11.7 months, so this is a role-mapped-but-unengaged economic buyer, not a confirmed one. The move is to convert Zsofia's own proposal to bring the head of procurement into a scoping demo into an actual introduction.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer described a demo-first process: an informal RFI via a Zycus demo (with the head of procurement invited), a sandbox session to explore the tool before award, then a formal RFP, targeting a signature that was originally end-2025. That process has repeatedly stalled: the RFP slipped from Aug 2025 to March 2026 and is now expected March 2027, and on 8 Jun 2026 Zsofia put the project on hold. Honda's procurement is outsourced to IBM (a 12-person Budapest team supporting UK-led indirect sourcing across 18 European countries), and the Next Step note flags Deloitte pushing an S2P+CLM agenda plus confirmed internal politics, so the real decision process is gated by internal alignment we do not yet have visibility into.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer described real, if informal, evaluation criteria on 18 Jul 2025: a sourcing platform that automates RFP/RFI templating and supplier scoring, supports collaboration with business stakeholders without repeated access requests, can either embed into Coupa or stand alone, and gives the head of procurement reporting visibility into audit stages (mandatory steps, supplier background and sanctioned-list screening). Zsofia also said the plan is a demo-first RFI, then an RFP whose requirements are already partly drafted. These criteria are buyer-stated but pre-RFP and not yet formalised in a document we have seen, so they are partial. Securing the draft RFP requirements would confirm them.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — Coupa is the incumbent and the central competitive reality: it is installed for P2P and reporting but unused and under-configured for sourcing, so the real fight is whether Honda re-optimises Coupa sourcing / does nothing versus adding a dedicated sourcing platform. As of 18 Jul 2025 the buyer had not decided whether to keep Coupa and said the RFP is 'not to challenge Coupa'. The strongest CURRENT threat is inertia / do-nothing: the project is on hold as of 8 Jun 2026 with the RFP slipped to March 2027. Deloitte is a live SI influence pushing an S2P+CLM agenda with a stated Coupa bias amid confirmed internal politics. No other named vendor is in evidence.
bullets.text: A competitor is pushing back — Coupa is the incumbent and the central competitive reality: it is installed for P2P and reporting but unused and under-configured for sourcing, so the real…
Buyer is leaning elsewhere — Buyer was positive about Zycus capability on the 18 Jul 2025 call but on 8 Jun 2026 put the project on hold with no current lean or Zycus-specific next step.

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS_Score 4 / AIS_Status Moderate (AI Curious). AI products are in scope but the only evidence is one introductory call from 18 Jul 2025 where the autonomous-negotiation agent was mentioned in passing; there is no buyer-side AI use-case validation, so the signal is under-evidenced rather than resistant.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: HONDA OF THE U.K. MANUFACTURING LIMITED — Qualified · $150K · closes 28 Feb

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 11 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (11), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

business_case.evidence: Vivid qualitative pain (250 manual sourcing events/year, email/SharePoint/Excel scoring, under-configured Coupa) but no cost, savings, cycle-time or ROI figure, and budget is unsized ('we do not know what to expect').

items.change: Owner changed from Rozalyn Orme to Claire Hudson
Claire Hudson re-introduced herself to Zsofia and raised RFP participation
Buyer replied the project is on hold and she will contact us when it continues
Close date pushed from 31 Jul 2026 to 28 Feb 2027; RFP now expected March

deal_movement.summary: The deal changed hands and then went on hold. Ownership moved from Rozalyn Orme to Claire Hudson on 13 May 2026; Claire re-introduced herself to Zsofia on 18 May 2026; Zsofia replied on 8 Jun 2026 that the project is paused and she will re-engage when it continues. On 30 Jun 2026 Claire pushed the close date from 31 Jul 2026 to 28 Feb 2027 and logged that the RFP is now expected March. No milestone (demo, RFP, commercials) has advanced since the 18 Jul 2025 discovery call.

north_star_verdict.math: Stage Qualified, forecast Pipeline. Created 14 Jul 2025, so ~11.7 months in Qualified with no stage advance. Close date reset from 31 Jul 2026 to 28 Feb 2027 on 30 Jun 2026, now ~240 days out, so the date is no longer the acute problem. One buyer touchpoint on record (discovery call 18 Jul 2025); the buyer's own plans (RFP Aug 2025, sign by year-end 2025, then RFP March 2026) all lapsed. On 8 Jun 2026 Zsofia replied that the project is paused and she will make contact when it continues, with the RFP now expected March 2027 and the demo still unscheduled. The deal is alive but stalled on a buyer-side hold, not cold: last activity 8 Jun 2026 (25 days ago).

Close date reset 31 Jul 2026 -> 28 Feb 2027 on 30 Jun 2026, removing the acute date pressure (SF OpportunityFieldHistory).

Next step records RFP now expected March and demo TBC (SF Next_Step__c), after Aug 2025 and March 2026 targets both lapsed.
Only one buyer call on record: discovery call 18 Jul 2025 (Avoma manifest, 1 call discovered/read); no demo ever delivered.
north_star_verdict.headline: The single discovery call on 18 Jul 2025 never converted to the promised demo, and the buyer put the whole project on hold on 8 Jun 2026, pushing the RFP to March 2027 and the close date out to 28 Feb 2027.

flags: Multi-thread beyond Zsofia to the named head of procurement she offered to bring into the demo; the deal is single-threaded and stalled.
Build a quantified cost-of-inaction case on the 250 manual sourcing events per year to create the compelling event the RFP lacks.
Neutralise Deloitte's Coupa-biased S2P agenda by presenting an integrated sourcing story that dovetails with Coupa reporting and the intake form.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a single-threaded, on-hold Qualified deal with strong product fit but no executive engagement or compelling event; the win levers are multi-threading to power, monetising the pain, and neutralising a Coupa-biased SI.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer was positive about Zycus capability on the 18 Jul 2025 call but on 8 Jun 2026 put the project on hold with no current lean or Zycus-specific next step.

As of 18 Jul 2025 the buyer had not decided whether to keep Coupa and said the RFP is 'not to challenge Coupa'. The strongest CURRENT threat is inertia / do-nothing: the project is on hold as of 8 Jun 2026 with the RFP slipped to March 2027. Deloitte is a live SI influence pushing an S2P+CLM agenda with a stated Coupa bias amid confirmed internal politics. No other named vendor is in evidence.

competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of 250 manual sourcing events per year and manual scoring so inertia becomes the expensive option, and anchor a compelling event tied to the March 2027 RFP.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: we are not utilizing Coupa for sourcing at all ... we did not put enough energy into the configuration customization

competitors.how_we_win: Position one integrated sourcing platform that embeds into or dovetails with Coupa reporting and the intake form; show automated scoring and Merlin autonomous negotiation Coupa's under-configured sourcing cannot deliver.

competitors.name: Deloitte (SI, S2P+CLM agenda)
competitors.quote: Deloitte has early engagement and pushing for s2p+clm and remove SAP. Bias towards Coupa. Deloitte confirmed internal politics but engaged and will work with us.
competitors.how_we_win: Engage the head of procurement directly to understand Deloitte's mandate and present a unified S2P + sourcing story that does not require Coupa-centricity.
